svg.title: Pay-per-call vs subscription for agent services (plain English)
svg.desc: Side-by-side comparison of two payment models. Left: pay per call. For each request, the service responds with "payment required", the user authorizes payment, retries, the service processes the payment through a payments processor, settlement completes, then the service is delivered. Right: subscription. The user has already paid for a period upfront. For each request, the service asks for proof of subscription, the user proves it, the service checks the subscriber list, gets a valid response, and delivers the service. Subscription has fewer moving parts per call and no payment processor in the loop, making it cheaper and faster for regular, repeated use.
svg.text: No payments processor in the loop
Subscription is cheaper and faster for regular, repeated use.
